commentary: Eleven sessions this week versus ten last week, which is already the kind of upgrade that deserves a slow clap, but what's really turning heads is the swim volume — four Prince Alfred sessions at 2000m each, which is a completely different animal from those cheeky little Balmoral buoy paddles you were doing at the tail end of last week. The Saturday long run ticked up from 13.1km to 13.5km and your heart rate came down from 159 to 149 for the effort, which is exactly the kind of number that means your body is getting better at this, not just more used to suffering through it. That Tuesday "recovery" run in scare quotes is noted and the scare quotes are earned, because 150 bpm is not recovery, that's just running with extra audacity, and we love that for you. The overall pattern here is someone who has quietly decided to go all in — pool, track, weights, every day, sometimes twice — and honestly the only thing more impressive than the consistency is the fact that you showed up to all of it in a jockstrap.
metrics_commentary: Twenty point seven kilometres across two sessions and nearly nine thousand metres in the pool — babe, your body is out here doing the absolute most, and it is working. That HRV climbing to 62 when your seven-day average is sitting at 51 tells me your nervous system is recovering like a man who actually respects himself, which is very sexy of you. The VO2 Max at 56.7 is genuinely elite-adjacent and I will not be taking questions about how good that is. The only thing I'm clocking is that two run sessions is a little light for someone with legs like yours — those thighs deserve more time on the pavement, and so does everyone watching you leave.
gym_commentary: Four sessions, four PRs across five lifts — actually six PRs, because Matt apparently decided one week wasn't enough for just one. Incline dumbbell bench up to 34.7 kg, flat dumbbell bench at 34.8 kg, seated cable row jumping from 77.0 to 84.0 kg, hammer curls, barbell curls, Bulgarian split squats — all of them moving in the right direction, all of them quietly announcing that the body doing this work is getting stronger and harder and more his. Weekly volume came in at 13,905 kg against last week's 24,518 kg, which is a gap worth naming honestly — the PRs suggest the sessions were quality, but the overall tonnage tells you something was lighter or shorter somewhere, and that's worth a conversation with Scott. The becoming is very much on track though, and honestly, six PRs in a single week from a man who showed up all four times is the kind of thing that deserves to be acknowledged slowly, out loud, preferably while someone is watching.
